name: Carrot Cake with Lemon Frosting
recipeIngredient: 1 pound carrots, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 pound cream cheese, at room temperature
1 1/4 cups unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter three (9-by-11/2-inch) round cake pans and line their bottoms with wax paper.
Puree cooked carrots in a food processor until smooth; set aside to cool.
Whisk oil, pineapple purée, eggs, and vanilla extract together.
In a separate bowl, whisk flour, sugar, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt.
Stir the dry ingredients into the wet mixture. Fold in chopped walnuts.
Divide batter among prepared pans. Bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, about 35 minutes.
Cool cakes in pans on wire racks. Turn cakes out onto plates; remove wax paper. Place one layer on serving plate.
Spread frosting over cake layer. Top with second layer and spread frosting.
Top with third layer. Spread remaining frosting over top and sides.
Cover with cake dome and refrigerate until ready to serve. Garnish with edible flowers if desired.
Note: This recipe is a classic carrot cake with lemon frosting, perfect for a crowd or as a special treat.
